title: Links, Images, References, Autolinks, and URL Safety · Transfer Build
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers links, images, references, autolinks, and url safety into a changed reference-aware accessible link and media model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of reference-aware accessible link and media model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Parse labels, destinations, optional titles, nesting, escaping, angle forms, and balanced delimiters under a named subset.
Normalize reference labels, detect duplicates, resolve forward references, preserve unresolved source, and report locations.
Classify relative, root-relative, fragment, mail, HTTP, data, and forbidden schemes before context-aware encoding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Link Grammar
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 9646 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve references and media through URL safety, integrity, and alternative-text decisions; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Link Grammar.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for parse labels, destinations, optional titles, nesting, escaping, angle forms, and balanced delimiters under a named subset. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Link Grammar, keep Link Grammar connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-link-integrity-1qtt8u
# Competency: ssg-link-integrity.
# Case 8424: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_link_integrity_1qtt8u(links: int = 9, resolved: int = 8, rejected: int = 1, unsafe_urls: int = 0, missing_alt_decisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1qtt8u = "1qtt8u-u8ttq1-8424"
    assert evidence_variant_1qtt8u
    evidence_axes_1qtt8u = (
        "reader1qtt8u",
        "source8424",
        "parseu8ttq1",
        "route1qtt8u8424",
        "artifact84241qtt8u",
        "recoveryu8ttq18424",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1qtt8u)) == 6
    if min(links, resolved, rejected) < 0:
        return False, links, "link-count"
    if resolved + rejected != links:
        return False, resolved, "link-reconciliation"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    if missing_alt_decisions > 0:
        return False, missing_alt_decisions, "media-alternative"
    assert resolved + rejected == links
    return True, resolved, "admitted-links-media"
